records.title: SSC Space Go external ICD, API lifecycle and five-site service architecture
records.summary: SSC Space Go publishes an external Ground-to-Ground Interface Control Document, version 2.9 dated June 2026, based on an OpenAPI specification. The document defines autonomous scheduling, TLE management, real-time TT&C access, S3 payload delivery, post-contact reports, metrics, error handling and a scheduler sandbox. It identifies five initial sites: Kiruna/Esrange, Alaska, Inuvik, Punta Arenas and Western Australia. The interface version history explicitly records breaking API changes and additions such as availability checks, booking horizons, service tiers, raw data distribution format and data-transfer progress reports.

confirmed_capabilities.simultaneous_reception: S and X with both polarizations according to ICD
confirmed_capabilities.dual_polarization: simultaneous dual-polarization downlink for compatible X/Ka spacecraft

confidence.basis: Official SSC external ICD and official SSC product publication

fields_to_confirm: TT&C gateway protocol details and authentication
S3 endpoint security and encryption profile
service-tier booking horizons and cancellation rules

sources.title: Fast, scalable ground access for small satellite operations

records.title: AWS Ground Station Seoul retirement, antenna inventory APIs and location lifecycle
records.summary: AWS documentation states that antenna resources in Seoul ceased to be supported on June 16, 2026. The current public location table lists eleven shared locations and maps each physical station to its AWS delivery region. AWS also exposes ListGroundStations, ListAntennas and ListGroundStationReservations APIs, but antenna inventory access is permission-gated: a customer must have the satellite onboarded to the site or have azimuth/elevation ephemeris permissions. Digital Twin stations mirror production station configurations, while Dedicated Antenna locations and capabilities are not publicly disclosed.

confirmed_capabilities.digital_twin: available in AWS regions where AWS Ground Station is available
confirmed_capabilities.dedicated_antenna_disclosure: locations and capabilities not publicly disclosed
confirmed_capabilities.data_region_mapping: physical station may be outside the AWS region receiving the data

confidence.basis: Current official AWS Ground Station documentation

fields_to_confirm: whether historical Seoul mission profiles remain visible
per-location antenna count and current reservations
service credits or contract treatment for location retirement
digital twin limits versus production hardware

records.title: AWS encrypted striped delivery and Kratos virtual front-end processing interfaces
records.summary: AWS Ground Station Agent delivery stripes satellite data near the antenna, encrypts it using a customer-provided AWS KMS key, transports the stripes across the AWS backbone, decrypts them at the customer EC2 receiver, applies forward error correction to recover dropped data and forwards the reconstructed stream to a configured IP/port. AWS documents that each dataflow terminates at a single EC2 receiver and is not inherently multi-AZ redundant. Separately, Kratos quantumFEP provides a software front-end processor between C2 and RF signal processing, including command/telemetry processing, recording, AES COMSEC, CCSDS processing, packet-level FEC and network gateway interfaces, with HTML5, API, REST or GEMS monitoring/control and deployment on bare metal, private cloud or public cloud.

confirmed_capabilities.aws_redundancy_note: single receiver per dataflow; not multi-AZ redundant by default

confidence.basis: Official AWS technical documentation and official Kratos product documentation

fields_to_confirm: compatibility evidence with specific modem vendors
